programme.desc: Documentary about a video and music retail store in Manhattan, New York City, that inspired a generation of cinephiles before it mysteriously closed its doors and sent its film archive to a small Sicilian village for safekeeping.

programme.desc: The former England cricket star and World Cup winner discusses the individuals that have inspired her in her life and career.

programme.desc: A reflective look at George W Bush's second term as president, which encompassed events including Hurricane Katrina and the global financial crisis.

programme.title: Costa Concordia: Chronicle of a Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary told from different perspectives of the sinking of the cruise ship, examining why it ran aground and how the chaotic evacuation took place.

programme.title: Discovering: Lee Marvin
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, who starred in movies including The Dirty Dozen, Point Blank and Paint Your Wagon and won an Oscar for Cat Ballou.

programme.title: The Fog of War
programme.desc: Former US Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara looks back over his career, explaining why, after initially supporting the decision to go to war in Vietnam, he eventually chose to speak out against the conflict - a decision that cost him his position. Documentary by acclaimed film-maker Errol Morris.

programme.title: The Commandant's Shadow
programme.desc: Documentary following the son of the man who was in charge of Auschwitz concentration camp as he confronts his father's involvement in the murder of more than a million Jews.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the legendary bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: Ibiza Narcos
programme.desc: Exploring how the Island was transformed into a vibrant party capital, fuelled by a dangerous and lucrative drugs trade that drew many criminals to its shores.

programme.title: Ibiza Narcos
programme.desc: A look at how British police clamping down on the illegal rave scene led to promoters and drug dealers seeing an opportunity to continue the party on Ibiza.

programme.title: Ibiza Narcos
programme.desc: A look at how cocaine was the dominant drug by the early 2000s, but greed and infighting led to bloodshed, leaving behind a dramatically transformed Ibiza.

programme.title: Kings of Coke
programme.desc: How a gang of Irish bank robbers from Montreal rose to become one of North America's most feared criminal organisations.

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.title: Discovering: Walter Matthau
programme.desc: Film experts reflect on the life and career of Oscar-winning actor Walter Matthau, who starred in the comedies Fortune Cookie, The Odd Couple and Grumpy Old Men.

programme.title: Discovering: Barbara Stanwyck
programme.desc: A profile of the American actress, who enjoyed a 60-year career in film and TV and best known for her strong roles in films such as Double Indemnity and Stella Dallas.

programme.title: Discovering: Gregory Peck
programme.desc: A profile of the hugely popular American actor who was nominated for five Academy Awards, winning for his role as Atticus Finch in 1962 film To Kill a Mockingbird.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director David Lynch, whose films include Eraserhead, Mulholland Drive, Wild at Heart, Blue Velvet and The Elephant Man.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of screenwriter and director Preston Sturges, whose films include Sullivan's Travels and The Great McGinty, for which he won an Oscar for original screenplay.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: The story of Allen Stanford's plan to revolutionise the game of cricket, which fell apart when he was exposed as a financial fraudster.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Now bankrupt, Allen Stanford spies a new opportunity in Montserrat. However, his new venture attracts the attention of US and UK law enforcement agencies.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Allen Stanford moves his operation to Antigua where he discovers the power of cricket in the Caribbean.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: In the heart of the Zambian bush lurks one of the last remaining strongholds for some of Africa's most endangered large carnivores.

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help o

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous pe

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of king penguins, filmed on the sub-Antarctic island of South Georgia over five months. The programme imagines what one penguin's life would be, following it during the challenging, awkward time between adolescence and adulthood. Presented by David Attenborough.

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster reveals how life has learnt to adapt quickly in the ever-changing volcanic landscape, resulting in species that exist nowhere else in the world. David investigates the driving forces behind their evolution, exploring the appearance and behaviour of remarkable creatures and vegetation including giant whale sharks, marine iguanas and seemingly normal dandelion seeds that grow into pl

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. Jumbo arrived at London Zoo in 1865 and was popular with the royal family, but was dangerously unhappy in captivity. In later life, he would be sold to PT Barnum's circus, becoming a celebrity before dying under mysterious circumstances. Attenborough examines Jumbo's r

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the ways different creatures hear and produce sound and how it can influence their decisions, using cutting-edge audio technology to reveal a new perspective and understanding of animal behaviour.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: From melodic songs to strange hums, David Attenborough shares the sounds that gibbons, lyrebirds, bull elk and more use to find a mate - and warn off the competition.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A look at how baby animals communicate in their quest for survival, using sound to beg and call for help in the early days of life.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist examines the world of flight, travelling across the globe to chart the evolutionary story of those creatures who seemingly defy the rules of gravity by taking to the air. Initially focusing on insects, Attenborough meets a descendant of the dragonfly, before examining the Atlas beetle, which tends to restrict flight to times when it needs a mate or a fresh supply of food. Mo

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. He also has a CGI-assisted encounter with the first true vertebrate flyer, the pterosaur, receives a flying lesson from a small flock of swans at Loch Lomond in Scotland and encounters the pl

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo -

programme.title: David Attenborough's Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect.

programme.title: Flying Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. Visiting sites around the world, from the Dorset coast to New Mexico, he explains how the reptiles evolved to defy the forces of gravity, and examines the features of their anatomy that can still be seen in present-day descendents. He also discovers why they became extinct after do

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenborough's career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. Other highlights include life in the frozen tundra of South Georgia, a colony of King Penguins and the world's largest migration of Hammerhead sharks i

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: In Kentucky, Pete creates a treehouse with 17-foot-high ceilings and open-frame timber rafters, allowing a couple with a love of tree-climbing to harness up and dangle from both inside and out.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson uproots an existing treehouse from the family-owned bed & breakfast Treehouse Point to build a custom-made treehouse as a housewarming gift for his daughter, Emily.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete pushes his design skills to create an owl-inspired tree house library in Charlotte, N.C.; evoking the face of the bird and using all sorts of curves, including rounded walls and semi-circle bookcases, the crew builds a one-of-a-kind masterpiece.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete and his team undertake a sticky challenge and reinvent the classic single-tree treehouse; constructing a magnificent and whimsical beehive-like structure soaring 30-feet high in a majestic Douglas fir.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark brings one of his biggest ideas to life -- a foldable steel cabin; the crew takes the cabin on its maiden voyage and unfolds it for the first time in the bone-yard, then they add a timber frame facade and a barn-wood interior.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe and his crew work through layers of architectural history to uncover an original pioneer home in Minor Hill, Tenn., while hearing stories from family members who lived in the cabin; Mark visits a beautifully restored log home.

programme.title: Second Chance Stage
programme.desc: On the eve of finale, the judges choose between six performances, including a jump rope team, dancers, stand-ups and a jazz crooner.

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda
programme.desc: Abner and Amanda share some of their favorite Persian recipes.

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda
programme.desc: Abner and Amanda prepare fresh and elevated dishes featuring eggs.

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda
programme.desc: A brunch inspired by travel and childhoods.

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda
programme.desc: Abner and Amanda start with osso buco, using beef shank rather than veal, add a traditional polenta, then bring it home with a popover recipe from Amanda's mother and fresh herb salad.

programme.title: In the Kitchen With Abner and Amanda
programme.desc: Abner's mom coaches him and Amanda on classic Cuban dishes.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: After a recent medical scare, Nicole is ready to live life to the fullest in a home by the water in Moorehead City, N.C.; a tight budget and unforeseen problems might put a hiccup in her plan for the perfect beach retreat.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple looking for a forever home for their family of four find a charming house that needs a major upgrade in Grays Harbor County in Washington; to save money, they plan to tackle most of the renovations themselves.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple that's relocating from the Washington, D.C.. area to Wildwood, N.J., finds the perfect beach house for their family of five; it needs serious renovating, and while they have a big budget, they also have a big wish list that means compromise.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: An Ohio couple is ready to trade in cold winters for the sunny skies of St. Petersburg, Fla.; they find a fixer-upper with potential to be exactly what they're looking for.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple looks for a cozy beach home in North Fork, N.Y. for their family of six to enjoy throughout the year.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: After discovering Avon, N.C., during a beach vacation, a family of four searches for a fixer-upper to call their own; once they've found their hidden gem, it's all hands on deck as renovations become a family affair involving multiple generations.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple sets out to fix up a property along the scenic Pacific Northwest coastline in Ocean Shores, Washington, so they can pass along childhood memories of growing up by the beach to their daughter.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: With two active children and busy jobs, Stacey and Doug are hoping to find a beach house in the Outer Banks of North Carolina to give their family a place to relax and recharge.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Maine Cabin Masters take on another Cobbosseecontee camp; Homeowner Walter White and his family dream of a new deck, a natural wood exterior, and an open layout to connect his porch and living room.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A group of brothers who inherited a rugged hunting cabin from their father and his friends enlist the team to restore it and preserve its legacy.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew kicks off a new waterside build, but a surprise call from the homeowners could change everything; with icy delays, a custom grill pit and help from Francis, this episode is packed with challenges, charm, and unexpected turns.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A surprise twist sends the crew into overdrive as they battle freezing temps to deliver this dream home; the team bands together to tackle weather setbacks, custom creations, and a pool table install.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A Penobscot tribe member summons the team for needed upgrades to her cabin; they have to work fast to beat the start of moose hunting season and to honor the land and Penobscot culture.
